name: QNI : Email Attachment with Executable Hidden in Double File Extensions
notes:
This rule triggers when a mail flow's attachment name contains at least two consecutive file extensions, and where one of them is associated to an executable.

This covers the case where an attacker appends a non-malicious file extension to the end of a malicious executable to bypass security services that block executable mail extensions. (e.g. virus.exe.txt, presentation.bat.pptx)

It also covers the case where an attacker sends a file with a non-executable file extension followed by an executable file extension. 
This can be used to trick users into opening malicious executables, as some operating systems like Windows hide the file extension when displaying files in their file system explorer. If the attacker can convince a user to download the file 'Report.doc.js', the operating system may display it as 'Report.doc'. (e.g. report.doc.js, newsletter.pdf.exe).

Note: The Application property can be tuned.
conditions_text: when a flow matches any of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Mail Applications in Flows
when the flow matches File Name is not N/A
when any of File Name match .+\..+\..+

when the flow matches REFERENCESETCONTAINS('Executable Extensions',REPLACEALL('.*\.(.+?)\..+?$',"file name",'$1')) OR REFERENCESETCONTAINS('Executable Extensions',REPLACEALL('.*\.(.+?)$',"file name",'$1')) AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY QNI : Email Attachment with Executable Hidden in Double File Extensions on flows which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when a flow matches any of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Mail Applications in Flows
AND when the flow matches File Name is not N/A
AND when any of File Name match .+\..+\..+

AND when the flow matches REFERENCESETCONTAINS('Executable Extensions',REPLACEALL('.*\.(.+?)\..+?$',"file name",'$1')) OR REFERENCESETCONTAINS('Executable Extensions',REPLACEALL('.*\.(.+?)$',"file name",'$1')) AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: QNI : Email Attachment with Multiple File Extensions Contains Executable
Event description: Detects when a mail flow's attachment name contains at least two consecutive file extensions, and where one of them is associated to an executable.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
